# Crypto Native AI Screener

Looking for a tool to keep up with the latest trends in AI and cryptocurrency? Our screener allows you to easily identify the current trend of specific coins.

We update our AI category list daily at 1:00 UTC, so you can stay up-to-date with the latest developments in the intersection of these two fields. Sort our table by trend streak, marketcap, exchange, derivatives, and more to find the information you need to make informed investment decisions.

Before making any trade, make sure to check the **Market Health Trends** page [here](https://coinrotator.app/market-health) to ensure you are always trading with the trend.


1.  [The Graph](https://coinrotator.app/coin/the-graph): Although The Graph does not have AI technology built-in, it can still be used as a data source for AI applications, offering secure and tamper-resistant data that can be trusted.
    
2.  [SingularityNET](https://coinrotator.app/coin/singularitynet): Is creating a marketplace for AI services where developers can create, share, and monetize their models. The platform allows users to have control over their data and directly interact with AI models, helping to democratize access to AI technology.
    
3.  [Fetch.ai](https://coinrotator.app/coin/fetch-ai): Combines smart contracts and multi-agent systems to deploy AI on its blockchain. Autonomous agents use AI algorithms for predictions, enabling various AI-powered apps such as supply chain management and decentralized marketplaces.
    
4.  [DeepBrain Chain](https://coinrotator.app/coin/deepbrain-chain): A decentralized AI computing platform that created a marketplace for buying and selling AI computing power.
    
5.  [Ocean Protocol](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ocean-protocol): A decentralized data exchange protocol that enables secure and privacy-preserving sharing of data assets.
    
6.  [ORAI Chain](https://coinrotator.app/coin/oraichain-token): Oraichain is a pioneering AI-powered oracle and AI Layer 1 that boasts a comprehensive AI ecosystem. It serves as the foundation for the development of smart contracts and dApps.
    
7.  [Numerai](https://coinrotator.app/coin/numeraire): A hedge fund that uses machine learning models to make investment decisions and pays contributors in its own cryptocurrency.
    
8.  [PHB](https://coinrotator.app/coin/phoenix-global): Phoenix Global hosts next-generation consumer-focused AI-powered application dApps with the aim of processing data in a privacy-preserving manner. It serves as both a computation layer and a bridge connecting on-chain protocols with off-chain data and AI models.

Related: [Big Data](https://coinrotator.app/category/big-data)

Check back frequently to see how the list updates, and start leveraging our **Crypto Native AI Screener** today.

-   [Algorand](https://coinrotator.app/coin/algorand): A blockchain platform that provides fast, secure, and scalable transactions for decentralized applications.
    
-   [Opulous](https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/opulous): A platform for the creation and trade of Music Fungible Tokens (MFTs) on the Algorand blockchain. The Opulous token (OPUL) serves as a governance token, a medium of exchange, and a store of value on the platform.

#Algorithmic Stablecoins - Decentralized ‘Non-Collateralized’ Stablecoins Coin Screener

Algorithmic stablecoins, also known as non-collateralized stablecoins, are a type of decentralized stablecoin that aim to maintain a stable market price through pre-programmed supply and demand matching. These stablecoins rely on predefined stabilization measures, which are often encoded in smart contracts on the Ethereum network.

-    DAI is a popular algorithmic stablecoin that is built on the Ethereum blockchain. It is issued and developed by the [Maker Protocol](https://coinrotator.app/coin/maker) and MakerDAO, which are both decentralized and autonomous. DAI is considered to be an effective stablecoin among the various options available in the market.

-    [Frax Protocol](https://coinrotator.app/coin/frax-share) is a decentralized and scalable algorithmic stablecoin system on Ethereum. It aims to provide a highly decentralized and scalable stablecoin for DeFi, using Frax stablecoin and Frax Shares token.

-    [Ampleforth](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ampleforth) is a decentralized stablecoin on Ethereum that maintains price stability through a flexible supply and a unique rebasing mechanism. AMPL, its stablecoin, is non-dilutive and elastic.

-    [Magic Internet Money](https://coinrotator.app/coin/magic-internet-money) is a popular algorithmic stablecoin found on crypto exchanges like Curve Finance, Uniswap, and PancakeSwap. Users can deposit interest-bearing assets and use them as collateral to borrow the stablecoin.

Related Categories: [DeFi](https://coinrotator.app/category/defi)

Source: 101Blockchains

This page is a coin screener for the category Algorithmic Stablecoins, which can be sorted by market cap, exchange, trend streak, and other data points. Before making any trades, always check the Market Health Screener to ensure you are trading with the trend.

## ISO 20022 Compliant Digital Assets Coin Screener

ISO 20022 is a global standard for electronic data interchange in the payment industry, using DLT technology and advanced messaging mechanisms. It is already supported by worldwide regulatory frameworks and is essential for any third-party interactions with banks.

Discover strong coins before they break out with our ISO 20022 Compliant Digital Assets Coin Screener. Sort by marketcap, exchange, derivatives, as well as trend streak (our proprietary measurement of crypto trends). Before making any trade, ensure that you check [Market Health Trends](https://coinrotator.app/market-health) to confirm that you are trading with the trend.

-   [Ripple](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ripple), a member of the ISO 20022 Standards Body, uses XRP on the XRPL as a bridge for cross-border settlements, among other purposes. At present, XRP can process about 1,500 transactions per second.
    
-   [Stellar](https://coinrotator.app/coin/stellar), also a member of the ISO 20022 Standards Body, uses Stellar Lumens as a bridge between the crypto and fiat worlds, with an emphasis on cross-currency conversions for individuals, similar to XRP. Although developers claim that XLM can handle up to 3,000 transactions per second, the processing times for real-world transactions have been historically slower.
    
-   [XinFin Network](https://coinrotator.app/coin/xdce-crowd-sale) is an EVM-compatible delegated proof-of-stake blockchain platform for enterprise digital asset networks.  XDC its native currency, and XinFin offers up to 2,000 transactions per second and finality in just 2 seconds. It's also compliant with ISO 20022 industry standards.
    
-   [Algorand](https://coinrotator.app/coin/algorand) is a ISO 20022 compliant Layer-1 blockchain that aims to address the issues of competitors such as Ethereum. The native token [ALGO](https://coinrotator.app/coin/algorand) can process around 1,300 transactions per second. Algorand promotes decentralization, but its [proof-of-stake](https://coinrotator.app/?category=Proof+of+Stake) consensus mechanism has raised concerns about whale dominance.
    
-   [Quant Network](https://coinrotator.app/coin/quant-network), also ISO 20022 compliant, is a blockchain protocol that solves interoperability issues between major blockchains like Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Ripple, as well as legacy networks, via its Overledger network. The protocol connects all blockchains via one comprehensive operating system and is EVM compatible. QNT is the native currency and is used by developers to create and operate dApps on the network.
    

Related Categories: [Proof of Stake](https://coinrotator.app/?category=Proof%20of%20Stake), [DeFi](https://coinrotator.app/?category=DeFi)

Source: [Supraoracles](https://supraoracles.com/academy/what-is-iso-20022-and-how-will-it-impact-the-crypto-industry/)
